title: GPT explained visually..
summary: A video by Caleb Writes Code providing a visual walkthrough of how GPT models work under the hood. The video covers core transformer components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, token embeddings, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections.
writeup: Caleb Writes Code presents a visual walkthrough of how GPT models operate internally, breaking down the transformer architecture into its component parts. The video covers self-attention and multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, token and positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The visual treatment maps how text moves through these stages, clarifying the role each component plays in the decoder stack.
